title: Equal COLA Act
plain_summary: The Equal COLA Act changes how cost-of-living adjustments work for federal workers under the Federal Employees Retirement System. Right now, these workers get smaller yearly increases than the actual inflation rate when prices go up a lot. This bill fixes that unfairness by making sure all federal retirees get the full inflation increase every year, no matter how high inflation gets. This means workers under both federal retirement systems will be treated the same way when their benefits are adjusted for rising costs.
last_action_text: ASSUMING FIRST SPONSORSHIP - Mr. Walkinshaw asked unanimous consent that he may hereafter be considered as the first sponsor of H.R. 491, a bill originally introduced by Representative Connolly, for t
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Equal COLA Act. COLA stands for cost-of-living adjustment, which means extra money added to retirement payments when prices go up.

sections.title: Section 2  Increase in COLA for FERS Annuities
sections.text: This section changes the rules for how much extra money federal workers get added to their retirement payments each year to help with rising costs. Right now, workers under two different retirement systems get different amounts of help. This law makes it so workers under the Federal Employees Retirement System get the same cost-of-living increases as workers under the Civil Service Retirement System. Starting each December first, retirement payments will go up by the same percentage that prices have gone up during the year. The increase will be rounded to the nearest tenth of one percent. These new rules will apply to all cost-of-living adjustments made after this law passes. They will also apply to all retirement payments, whether they started before, on, or after this law takes effect.
